/**
 * TEC-623 All-in-One Seed and Test Script
 *
 * Creates comprehensive test data for duplicate barrel detection via lot variation resolution
 * Follows the established seeding architecture used by android sync and device sync errors
 *
 * Usage:
 *   npx ts-node scripts/seed-and-test-tec-623.ts
 *
 * What this script creates:
 * - Canonical lots for testing (e.g., 25G29AB.10)
 * - Lot variations mapping to canonical lots (e.g., 25G29AB → 25G29AB.10)
 * - Test barrels on canonical lots
 * - Warehouse job for inventory check context
 * - AndroidSyncInbox records that will trigger duplicate detection

context.snippet:
WITH BarrelStateAtTime AS (
        WITH RankedChanges AS (
          SELECT
            barrel,
            "locationId",
            "changedAt",
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
              PARTITION BY barrel
              ORDER BY "changedAt" DESC
            ) as rn
          FROM "BarrelAudit"
          WHERE "changedAt" <= $${queryParams.length + 1}
        ),
        LatestAuditState AS (
          SELECT
            barrel as "id",
            "locationId" as "audit_locationId"
          FROM RankedChanges
          WHERE rn = 1
        )
        SELECT
          b.id,
          COALESCE(a."audit_locationId", b."locationId") as "locationId",
          CASE
            WHEN a."id" IS NOT NULL THEN 'AUDIT'
            ELSE 'CURRENT'
          END as "dataSource",
          b."serialNumber",
          b."barrelSize",
          b."cooperageCode",
          b.recipe,
          b."fillDate",
          b."char",
          b."seasoning",
          b."toast",

    return {
      id: request.id,
      requestType: request.requestType,
      title: request.title,
      description: request.description,

        id: request.requestedBy,
        name: request.requestedBy, // In a real implementation, you'd join with User table

      },
      requestedAt: request.requestedAt,
      priority: request.priority,
      status: request.status,
      estimatedImpact: request.estimatedImpact,
      affectedRecords: request.affectedRecords,
      requestData: request.requestData,
      requiredApprovals: request.requiredApprovals,
      currentApprovals: request.currentApprovals,
      approvers: request.approvers.map((approver: any) => ({
        id: approver.id,
        user: {
          id: approver.approverId,
          name: approver.approverId, // In a real implementation, you'd join with User table
          email: `${approver.approverId}@company.com`, // Placeholder
        },
        status: approver.status,
        respondedAt: approver.respondedAt,
        comments: approver.comments,
      })),
      comments: request.comments.map((comment: any) => ({

          name: comment.authorId, // In a real implementation, you'd join with User table

import { PrismaService } from "../prisma/prisma.service";

  page: number;
  pageSize: number;
}

type SortDirection = "asc" | "desc";
type OrderByConfig = Record<string, SortDirection>;
type FiltersConfig = Record<string, string | number | boolean>;

const EXPECTED_ERROR_PATTERNS = [
  // Transaction conflicts (expected in concurrency tests)
  /Transaction failed due to a write conflict or a deadlock/,
  /PrismaClientKnownRequestError.*deadlock/,
  
  // Business logic errors (expected in error handling tests)
  /Storage location is already occupied/,
  /Storage location does not exist/,
  /Lot with number.*not found/,
  /Recipe not found for lot/,
  /Recipe data not found for lot/,
  /EventReason not found/,
  /Invalid Inspection payload/,
  /BarrelNoLabel/,
  /HoldingLocation.*not found/,
  /customerCode not found in recipe data/,
  /Customer not found for customerCode/,
  
  // Processing errors (expected in background processing tests)
  /Error processing record/,
  /Android sync transaction failed/,
  /Serializable transaction failed/,
  /Failed to log error to database/,
  /Unknown argument `inboxDeviceName`/,
  /No 'AndroidSyncInbox' record.*needed to inline the relation/,
  
  // NestJS internal logs
  /\[PrismaService\]/,
  /\[AndroidSyncInboxListener\]/,
  /\[BarrelEventService\]/,
  
  // NestJS startup logs (suppress during tests)
  /\[Nest\] Mapped.*route/,
  /\[Nest\].*Controller/,
  /\[Nest\] Nest application successfully started/,
  /\[Nest\] GraphQLModule dependencies initialized/,
  /\[Nest\].*Module dependencies initialized/,
  /\[Nest\] Authenticating request/,
  /\[Nest\] Skipping authentication/,
  /\[Nest\] Mock user applied/,
  
  // Security middleware logs
  /\[SecurityHeadersMiddleware\]/,
  
  // Connection pool and timeout errors (common in CI with limited resources)
  /Timed out fetching a new connection from the connection pool/,
  /Unable to start a transaction in the given time/,
  /Transaction API error/,
  /connection pool timeout/,
  /Current connection pool timeout/,
  /connection limit/,
  /P2024/,
  /Read-only transaction failed after/,
];

/**
 * Patterns for logs that should always be shown (even if they match error patterns)
 */

import { registerEnumType } from "@nestjs/graphql";

export enum EnumEventTypeName {
  Entry = "Entry",
  Withdrawal = "Withdrawal",
  Rework = "Rework",
  Reject = "Reject",
  TransferIn = "TransferIn",
  TransferOut = "TransferOut",
  Sample = "Sample",
  DumpForRegauge = "DumpForRegauge",
  Relocation = "Relocation",
  Fill = "Fill",
  Quality = "Quality",
  Inspection = "Inspection",
  InventoryCheck = "InventoryCheck",
  InventoryCheckDisplaced = "InventoryCheckDisplaced",
  InventoryRemoval = "InventoryRemoval",
  InventoryAddition = "InventoryAddition",
  InventoryUploadAdded = "InventoryUploadAdded",
  InventoryUploadUpdated = "InventoryUploadUpdated",
  InventoryUploadRemoved = "InventoryUploadRemoved",
  InventoryUploadReconciliationDuplicateRemoved = "InventoryUploadReconciliationDuplicateRemoved",
  InventoryUploadDisplaced = "InventoryUploadDisplaced",
  /// Initial label print for a barrel
  LabelPrinted = "LabelPrinted",
  /// Subsequent label print (reprint) for existing barrel
  LabelReprinted = "LabelReprinted",
  /// Barrel verification scan during a verification job (VJOB)
  Verified = "Verified",
}

registerEnumType(EnumEventTypeName, {
  name: "EnumEventTypeName",
});

context.snippet:
InventoryUploadAdded: StorageEventCategory.ENTRY,

  // Withdrawal events - barrels leaving storage
  Withdrawal: StorageEventCategory.WITHDRAWAL,
  InventoryUploadRemoved: StorageEventCategory.WITHDRAWAL,

  // Transfer events - barrels transferred in
  TransferIn: StorageEventCategory.TRANSFER_IN,

  // Dump events - barrels dumped/destroyed
  DumpForRegauge: StorageEventCategory.DUMP,
  InventoryUploadReconciliationDuplicateRemoved: StorageEventCategory.DUMP,

  // Excluded events (return null - not included in storage aggregation)
  InventoryCheck: null,
  InventoryRemoval: null,
  InventoryAddition: null,
  Inspection: null,
  Quality: null,
  Sample: null,
  Relocation: null,

  /Transaction failed due to a write conflict or a deadlock/,
  /PrismaClientKnownRequestError.*deadlock/,
  
  // Business logic errors (expected in error handling tests)
  /Storage location is already occupied/,
  /Storage location does not exist/,
  /Lot with number.*not found/,
  /Recipe not found for lot/,
  /Recipe data not found for lot/,
  /EventReason not found/,
  /Invalid Inspection payload/,
  /BarrelNoLabel/,
  /HoldingLocation.*not found/,
  /customerCode not found in recipe data/,
  /Customer not found for customerCode/,
  
  // Processing errors (expected in background processing tests)
  /Error processing record/,
  /Android sync transaction failed/,
  /Serializable transaction failed/,
  /Failed to log error to database/,
  /Unknown argument `inboxDeviceName`/,
  /No 'AndroidSyncInbox' record.*needed to inline the relation/,
  
  // NestJS internal logs
  /\[PrismaService\]/,
  /\[AndroidSyncInboxListener\]/,
  /\[BarrelEventService\]/,
  
  // NestJS startup logs (suppress during tests)
  /\[Nest\] Mapped.*route/,
  /\[Nest\].*Controller/,
  /\[Nest\] Nest application successfully started/,
  /\[Nest\] GraphQLModule dependencies initialized/,
  /\[Nest\].*Module dependencies initialized/,
  /\[Nest\] Authenticating request/,
  /\[Nest\] Skipping authentication/,
  /\[Nest\] Mock user applied/,
  
  // Security middleware logs
  /\[SecurityHeadersMiddleware\]/,
  
  // Connection pool and timeout errors (common in CI with limited resources)
  /Timed out fetching a new connection from the connection pool/,
  /Unable to start a transaction in the given time/,
  /Transaction API error/,
  /connectio

You have access to tools that allow you to query the database, search documentation, and analyze warehouse data.

## Response Formatting (IMPORTANT)
- ALWAYS put a blank line between paragraphs and between distinct thoughts

context.snippet:
/**
 * Integration tests for distributed cache invalidation
 *
 * These tests verify that:
 * 1. Manual cache invalidation works correctly in cached services
 * 2. Cache invalidation events are distributed via Redis pub/sub
 * 3. All instances receive and process invalidation events
 * 4. Cache consistency is maintained across multiple instances
 *
 * NOTE: Prisma v6 does not support $use middleware API. Cache invalidation
 * must be performed manually in cached services (storage-location.service.ts,
 * event-reason.service.ts, event-type.service.ts) when data is modified.
 *
 * NOTE: These tests require a running Redis instance.
 * Set SKIP_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true to skip if Redis is not available.
 */

import { Test, TestingModule } from "@nestjs/testing";
import { PrismaService } from "../prisma/prisma.service";
import { CachedStorageLocationService } from "../storageLocation/cached-storage-location.service";
import { CachedEventReasonService } from "../eventReason/cached-event-reason.service";

## Database Schema
The database uses PostgreSQL with a Prisma schema. Key tables include:
- **"Barrel"** - Core barrel tracking (disposition field indicates status: 'Filled', 'InStorage', 'InHolding', 'Hold', 'PendingDisposition', 'Withdrawn', 'Dumped', etc.)
- **"BarrelOwnership"** - Tracks which customers own which barrels (use this for customer inventory queries!)
- **"Customer"** - Customer entities (id, name, etc.)
- **"Lot"** - Lot management

context.snippet:
* AndroidSyncDto Test Suite
 *
 * This file tests the Data Transfer Objects (DTOs) used for Android sync API validation.
 *
 * TEST COVERAGE:
 *
 * 1. AndroidSyncEventDto - Individual sync data record validation
 *    - ✅ Validates required fields: eventsId, barrelSN, eventType
 *    - ✅ Validates optional fields: locationString, operator, createdAt
 *    - ✅ Tests proper JSON entity payload handling
 *    - ✅ Validates date/timestamp transformation and parsing
 *    - ✅ Handles missing optional fields gracefully
 *    - ✅ Validates string field length and format constraints
 *    - ✅ Tests field type conversion (strings, dates, JSON)
 *
 * 2. AndroidSyncMetadataDto - Device metadata validation
 *    - ✅ Validates required deviceName field
 *    - ✅ Validates optional appVersion field
 *    - ✅ Tests proper string validation and sanitization
 *    - ✅ Handles missing optional metadata gracefully
 *    - ✅ Validates device identification requirements
 *
 * 3. AndroidSyncDto - Complete sync payload validation
 *    - ✅ Validates required data array (must not be empty)
 *    - ✅ Validates optional metadata object
 *    - ✅ Tests nested object validation (data items and metadata)
 *    - ✅ Validates array constraints and minimum length requirements
 *    - ✅ Tests complex nested validation scenarios
 *    - ✅ Handles partial payload validation (missing metadata)
 *    - ✅ Validates complete end-to-end payload structure
 *
 * VALIDATION SCENARIOS TESTED:
 *    - ✅ Valid complete payloads with all fields
 *    - ✅ Partial payloads with only required fields
 *    - ✅ Invalid payloads with missing required fields
 *    - ✅ Invalid field types and format validation
 *    - ✅ Edge cases: empty arrays, null values, undefined fields
 *    - ✅ Date parsing and transformation edge cases
 *    - ✅ JSON payload validation and structure
 *
 * CLASS-VALIDATOR INTEGRATION:
 *    - ✅ @IsNotEmpty validation for required fields
 *    - ✅ @IsOptional handling for optional fields
 *    -
